differentials.raw: bleeding from EAC ← disruption of tympanic membrane

differentials.name: persistent vertigo
differentials.raw: persistent vertigo (benign paroxysmal positional vertigo resolves in 6-12 months, perilymphatic fistula, cupulolithiasis = otolith detachment, trauma to semicircular canals)

differentials.name: facial (cranial nerve VII) nerve palsy in 50% (injury in IAC); less frequent spontaneous recovery because of disruption of nerve fibers

context: Prevalence:   involved in 15% of skull-base fractures √  air-fluid level in sinuses + mastoid √  axial thin-slice high-resolution CT for best delineation of fractures √  water-soluble intrathecal contrast material for CSF fistula Temporal Bone Fracture Frequency:   14-22% of skull fractures Mechanism:   motor vehicle crash (45-47%), fall (31-33%), assault (11-12%) Cause of conductive hearing loss of temporal bone fracture: ›  commonly incus injury: (a)  incudostapedial joint subluxation (b)  malleoincudal subluxation (c)  incus dislocation (d)  dislocation of the malleoincudal complex ›  less
